%==========================================================%
%  MAIN.TEX — Document entry point
%==========================================================%
%
%  This is the ONLY file you should compile (set it as the
%  main document in Overleaf: menu > Settings > Main document).
%
%  You should not need to edit this file at all for a normal
%  submission. It simply assembles the other files in the
%  correct order:
%
%    sections/preamble.tex   -> journal formatting (DO NOT EDIT)
%    user/usercode.tex       -> your own extra packages/macros
%    user/userdata.tex       -> title, authors, abstract, keywords
%    sections/cover.tex      -> title page layout (DO NOT EDIT)
%    user/content.tex        -> the body of your manuscript
%    user/biblio.bib         -> your reference database (BibTeX)
%
%  Everything you are expected to fill in lives under the
%  'user/' folder. Files under 'sections/' and 'references/'
%  define the journal's required format and should be left
%  untouched so every submission is typeset consistently.
%
%==========================================================%

% Include the preamble settings. Do not modify this file.
\input{sections/preamble}

% 'usercode.tex' is for any additional LaTeX packages or commands you need.
% You can add your custom LaTeX code in this file.
\input{user/usercode}

% 'userdata.tex' contains the metadata for your paper (title, authors, etc.).
\input{user/userdata}

% Begin the document.
\begin{document}
\doublespacing
% Include the cover information. This file should not be modified.
\input{sections/cover}

% 'content.tex' is where you will write the body of your paper.
\input{user/content}

%----------------------------------------------------------%
% BIBLIOGRAPHY
%----------------------------------------------------------%
% This template uses BibTeX with a customized IEEEtran style
% (IEEEtranDOI.bst, stored in references/) that additionally
% prints the DOI of each reference. Do not rename or move
% 'IEEEtranDOI.bst' — if you do, update the path below to match.
%
% Add your own references to 'user/biblio.bib' in BibTeX format
% and cite them in content.tex with \cite{yourkey}.
%
% 'IEEEabrv' below is not a file in this project: it is a
% standard bibliography-string file that ships with every full
% TeX Live installation (including Overleaf's), providing
% abbreviated IEEE journal names. You do not need to add it
% yourself and should not remove it from the line below.
\bibliographystyle{references/IEEEtranDOI}
\bibliography{IEEEabrv,user/biblio}

% End of the document.
\end{document}
